name: threedi-modelchecker
documentation:
# threedi-modelchecker

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/threedi-modelchecker.svg
:target: https://pypi.org/project/threedi-modelchecker/
```

```{image} https://github.com/nens/threedi-modelchecker/actions/workflows/test.yml/badge.svg
:alt: Github Actions status
:target: https://github.com/nens/threedi-modelchecker/actions/workflows/test.yml
```

A tool to verify the correctness of a 3Di schematisation.

It asserts the correctness of a 3Di schematisation and provides detailed
information about any potential errors in it.

This package only work against a specific schematisation version. Use <https://pypi.org/project/threedi-schema/> to upgrade
a schematisation version. See also the 3Di documentation at <https://docs.3di.lizard.net/en/stable/d_before_you_begin.html#database-overview>

Note that the name "modelchecker" might be slightly confusing: the thing that is checked is a schematisation, and not
a threedimodel. A schematisation is built interactively by the user and this schematisation will be converted to a
3Di-Model which can be used in simulations.

## Installation

> pip install threedi-modelchecker

Note that raster checks will be skipped unless GDAL is available.
`threedi-modelchecker` is also integrated into the ThreediToolbox Qgis plugin: <https://github.com/nens/ThreeDiToolbox>

## Example

The following code sample shows how you can use the modelchecker to run all configured
checks and print an overview of all discovered errors:

```
from threedi_modelchecker.exporters import format_check_results
from threedi_modelchecker import ThreediModelChecker
from threedi_schema import ThreediDatabase

sqlite_file = "<Path to your sqlite file>"
database = ThreediDatabase(sqlite_file)

model_checker = ThreediModelChecker(database)
for check, error in model_checker.errors(level="WARNING"):
    print(format_check_results(check, error))
```

## Command-line interface

Use the modelchecker from the command line as follows:

```
threedi_modelchecker check -s path/to/model.sqlite -l warning
```

By default, WARNING and INFO checks are ignored. To skip the beta features check,
add the --allow-beta flag.

## Development

A docker image has been created for easy development. It contains an postgis
server with an empty 3Di database to allow for easy testing.

Build the image:

> docker-compose build

Run the tests:

> docker-compose run modelchecker pytest

See [Creating revisions](https://github.com/nens/threedi-schema/blob/master/threedi_schema/migrations/README.rst) for
instructions on how to change the 3Di model schematisation.

## Release

Make sure you have [zestreleaser] installed.

> fullrelease

When you created a tag, make sure to upload it to [pypi].

[pypi]: https://pypi.org/project/threedi-modelchecker/
[zestreleaser]: https://zestreleaser.readthedocs.io/en/latest/

functions.name: threedi_modelchecker.tests.conftest.threedi_db
documentation:
Fixture which yields a empty 3di database

A global Session object is configured based on database type. This allows
the factories to operate on the same session object. See:
https://factoryboy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/orms.html#managing-sessions

functions.name: threedi_modelchecker.tests.conftest.session
documentation:
Fixture which yields a session to an empty 3di database.

At the end of the test, all uncommitted changes are rolled back. Never
commit any transactions to the session! This will persist the changes
and affect the upcoming tests.

```
return

sqlalchemy.orm.session.Session
```

functions.name: threedi_modelchecker.exporters.order_checks
documentation:
Alphabetically order checks so that they will consistently be ordered the same.
This orders first by error code, and then checks with the same error code are
sorted alphabetically by description. Checks are not sorted by level, because
in general, checks with the same error code have the same level.

This makes Github PR requests a lot clearer.
check overview export functions

Convert the sqlalchemy column type to allowed sqlite data types

Returns the value similar as the sqlite 'typeof' function.
Raises TypeError if the column type is unknown.
See <https://www.sqlite.org/datatype3.html>

Base class for all checks.

A Check defines a constraint on a specific column and its table.
One can validate if the constrain holds using the method `get_invalid()`.
This method will return a list of rows (as named_tuples) which are invalid.

documentation: Specify a sqlalchemy.orm.Query object to return invalid instances

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.base.ForeignKeyCheck
documentation:
Check all values in `column` are in `reference_column`.

Null values are ignored.

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.base.UniqueCheck
documentation:
Check all values in `column` are unique

Null values are ignored.

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.base.AllEqualCheck
documentation: Check all values in `column` are the same, including NULL values.

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.base.NotNullCheck
documentation: "Check all values in `column` that are not null

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.base.TypeCheck
documentation:
Check all values in `column` that are of the column defined type.

Null values are ignored.

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.base.GeometryCheck
documentation:
Check all values in `column` are a valid geometry.

Null values are ignored.

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.base.GeometryTypeCheck
documentation:
Check all values in `column` are of geometry type in defined in
`column`.

Null values are ignored

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.base.EnumCheck
documentation:
Check all values in `column` are within the defined Enum values of
`column`.

Unexpected values are values not defined by its enum_class.

Null values are ignored

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.base.RangeCheck
documentation:
Check to if all values are within range [min_value, max_value]

Use left_inclusive and right_inclusive to specify whether the min/max values
themselves should be considered valid. By default they are both considered
valid.

functions.name: threedi_modelchecker.tests.test_checks_other.test_cross_section_same_configuration
documentation:
This test checks two cross-sections on a channel against each other; they should both be open or both be closed.
In this test, the first cross-section has been set to always be open.
Therefore, the channel should be invalid when the second cross-section is closed, and valid when it is open.
cases to test: tabulated and non tabulated, everything else is covered above

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.raster.Context
documentation: interface_cls.is_valid_geotiff returns False if the dataset is empty
(such as when the column doesn't reference a file) or the dataset is not a valid geotiff.
It's used on every raster check which reads the raster file in any way,
to prevent any other code in the check from executing on a bad file.
On such datasets, RasterExistsCheck and/or RasterIsValidCheck will be raised.

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.raster.BaseRasterCheck
documentation:
Baseclass for all raster checks.

Because these checks are different on local and server systems, subclasses may
implement 2 methods: is_valid_local and/or is_valid_local.

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.raster.RasterExistsCheck
documentation:
Check whether a file referenced in given Column exists.

In order to perform this check, the SQLAlchemy session requires a
`model_checker_context` attribute, which is set automatically by the
ThreediModelChecker and contains either a LocalContext or
ServerContextinstance.

Check whether a raster has values outside of provided range.

methods.name: threedi_modelchecker.model_checks.ThreediModelChecker.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the model checker.

Optionally, supply the context of the model check:

- "context_type": "local" or "server", default "local"
- "raster_interface": a threedi_modelchecker.interfaces.RasterInterface subclass
- "base_path": (only local) path where to look for rasters (defaults to the db's directory)
- "available_rasters": (only server) a dict of raster_option -> raster url

documentation: Returns a list of declared models

methods.name: threedi_modelchecker.model_checks.ThreediModelChecker.errors
documentation:
Iterates and applies checks, returning any failing rows.

By default, checks of WARNING and INFO level are ignored.

```
return

Tuple of the applied check and the failing row.
```

methods.name: threedi_modelchecker.model_checks.ThreediModelChecker.checks

```

```

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.timeseries.TimeseriesExistenceCheck
documentation: Check that an empty timeseries has not been provided.

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.timeseries.TimeSeriesEqualTimestepsCheck
documentation:
Check that the timesteps in all timeseries in a column are equal.

This checks the timesteps for all timeseries in a column against the timesteps in the first
timeseries in that column. Consequently, if the first timeseries is wrong, all the other timeseries
in that column will raise a warning.
This check does not compare timeseries between different columns; for that, FirstTimeSeriesEqualTimestepsCheck
is used.

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.timeseries.FirstTimeSeriesEqualTimestepsCheck
documentation:
Check that the timesteps in the first timeseries in the boundary condition columns are equal, if they both exist.

This is used in conjunction with TimeSeriesEqualTimestepsCheck to confirm that the timeseries in all the boundary
conditions have the same timesteps. If each timeseries within each column has the same timesteps, and the first
timeseries of each column matches timesteps with the first timeseries of each other column, then the timesteps of
all the timeseries must be the same.

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.timeseries.TimeseriesRowCheck
documentation: Check that each record in a timeserie contains 2 elements

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.timeseries.TimeseriesTimestepCheck
documentation: Check that each record in a timeserie starts with an integer >= 0

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.timeseries.TimeseriesValueCheck
documentation: Check that each record in a timeserie ends with a float and is not an invalid or empty string

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.timeseries.TimeseriesIncreasingCheck
documentation: The timesteps in a timeseries should increase

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.timeseries.TimeseriesStartsAtZeroCheck
documentation: The timesteps in a timeseries should start at 0

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.other.CorrectAggregationSettingsExist

Check the cross-sections on the object are either all open or all closed.

Check that when use_0d_flow in global settings is configured to 1 or to

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.other.ConnectionNodes
documentation:
Check that all connection nodes are connected to at least one of the
following objects:
- Culvert
- Channel
- Pipe
- Orifice
- Pumpstation
- Weir

documentation:
---
param end_node: column name of the end node

---

Check that the distance between `start_node` and `end_node` is at least
`min_distance`. The coords will be transformed into (the first entry) of
ModelSettings.epsg_code.

The query makes use of the SpatialIndex so we won't have to calculate the
distance between all connection nodes.

Check that the distance between connection nodes is above a certain
threshold

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.other.ChannelManholeLevelCheck

methods.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.other.ChannelManholeLevelCheck.__init__
documentation:
---
param level: severity of the check, defaults to CheckLevel.INFO. Options are
---

in checks.base.CheckLevel
\:param nodes_to_check: whether to check for manholes at the start of the channel
or at the end of the channel. Options are "start" and "end", defaults to "start"

methods.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.other.ChannelManholeLevelCheck.get_invalid
documentation:
This query does the following:
channel_with_cs_locations       : left join between cross_sections and channels, to get a table containing
                                  all cross-sections and the channels they lie on
channels_with_manholes          : join between channel_with_cs_locations and manholes, to get all channels with
                                  a manhole on the channel's start node if self.nodes_to_check == "start", or all
                                  channels with a manhole on the channel's end node if self.nodes_to_check == "start".
channels_manholes_level_checked : filter the query on invalid entries; that is, entries where the cross-section
                                  reference level is indeed lower than the manhole bottom level. having is used instead
                                  of filter because the query being filtered is a aggregate produced by groupby.

Check that the reference_level of a channel is higher than or equal to the bottom_level of a manhole
connected to the channel as measured at the cross-section closest to the manhole. This check runs if the
manhole is on the channel's starting node.

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.other.OpenChannelsWithNestedNewton
documentation:
Checks whether the model has any closed cross-section in use when the
NumericalSettings.use_nested_newton is turned off.

See https://github.com/nens/threeditoolbox/issues/522

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.other.LinestringLocationCheck
documentation:
Check that linestring geometry starts / ends are close to their connection nodes

This allows for reversing the geometries. threedi-gridbuilder will reverse the geometries if
that lowers the distance to the connection nodes.

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.other.BoundaryCondition1DObjectNumberCheck
documentation: Check that the number of connected objects to 1D boundary connections is 1.

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.other.SpatialIndexCheck
documentation: Checks whether a spatial index is present and valid

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.other.PotentialBreachStartEndCheck
documentation: Check that a potential breach is exactly on or >=1 m from a linestring start/end.

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.other.PotentialBreachInterdistanceCheck
documentation: Check that a potential breaches are exactly on the same place or >=1 m apart.

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.other.PumpStorageTimestepCheck
documentation: Check that a pumpstation will not empty its storage area within one timestep

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.other.SurfaceNodeInflowAreaCheck
documentation: Check that total inflow area per connection node is no larger than 10000 square metres

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.other.PerviousNodeInflowAreaCheck
documentation: Check that total inflow area per connection node is no larger than 10000 square metres

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.other.InflowNoFeaturesCheck

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.other.NodeSurfaceConnectionsCheck

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.other.FeatureClosedCrossSectionCheck

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.other.DefinedAreaCheck
documentation: Check if the value in the 'area' column matches the surface area of 'geom'

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.other.BetaColumnsCheck

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.other.BetaValuesCheck
documentation: Check that no beta features were used in the database

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.checks.other.AllPresentVegetationParameters
documentation: Check if all or none vegetation values are defined in the CrossSectionLocation table

classes.name: threedi_modelchecker.config.Config

methods.name: threedi_modelchecker.config.Config.iter_checks
documentation: Iterate over checks with at least 'level'

Collection of checks

Some checks are generated by a factory. These are usually very generic
checks which apply to many columns, such as foreign keys.
